title: Number of operations to make network connected

content.problemStatement: You are given a graph with n vertices and m edges. You can remove one edge from anywhere and add that edge between any two vertices in one operation. Find the minimum number of operations that will be required to make the graph connected. If it is not possible to make the graph connected, return -1.
content.inputFormat: The input will contain two parameters: n (the number of vertices) and connections (a 2D array representing the edges in the graph).
content.outputFormat: The output will be the minimum number of operations required to make the graph connected. If it's not possible to make the graph connected, return -1.

solution.approach: Use Disjoint Set Union to keep track of connected components and calculate the minimum number of operations required to connect all components.
